title: System Settings
subtitle: customize, control, manage!
overview: This session introduces students to System Settings in Windows. Students will learn how to personalize computers, manage display and sound settings, connect devices, and understand important system controls safely.

images._note: Add screenshots from Windows Settings, classroom activities, and device setup examples.

Sound Settings — speaker and microphone control

activity.title: Customize Your Computer Activity
steps: Open Windows Settings using Windows + I

quiz.explanation: Windows + I quickly opens System Settings.
Display Settings allow users to adjust brightness and resolution.

quiz.q: Which setting is used to connect internet wirelessly?
quiz.explanation: Wi-Fi settings help connect computers to the internet.
Bluetooth connects wireless devices like headphones and keyboards.
Personalization settings allow wallpaper and theme changes.

quiz.q: What is used to control computer sound?
quiz.explanation: Volume controls speaker and microphone sound levels.

options: To customize and manage computer features
quiz.explanation: System Settings help users manage and personalize computers.
Update & Security helps keep computers safe and updated.

quiz.q: What happens if volume is muted?
quiz.explanation: Muted volume means sound output is turned off.
Personalization changes the look and feel of the computer.

quiz.q: Which option helps save battery life?
quiz.explanation: Battery Saver Mode helps reduce power usage.
Resolution controls how clear and sharp items appear on screen.

quiz.q: Which device can connect through Bluetooth?
quiz.explanation: Bluetooth is commonly used for wireless headphones and speakers.

quiz.q: What should students avoid changing without permission?
quiz.explanation: Important system settings should only be changed carefully or with permission.

quiz.q: What is the purpose of Date and Time settings?
options: To manage correct system clock and calendar
quiz.explanation: Date and Time settings keep the computer clock accurate.
